sentence: The new policy was brought ______ to reduce workplace accidents.
explanation: El phrasal verb <bring in> significa introducir algo nuevo, como una política o norma.
tip: A new policy was introduced to improve safety.

sentence: She had to fall ______ her savings when she lost her job.
explanation: El phrasal verb <fall back on> significa recurrir a algo como último recurso.
tip: She used her savings as a backup when she had no income.

sentence: The team managed to pull ______ a win despite being behind.
explanation: El phrasal verb <pull off> significa lograr algo difícil o inesperado.
tip: They achieved something difficult — they won against the odds.

sentence: He needs to cut ______ on his spending if he wants to save money.
explanation: El phrasal verb <cut back on> significa reducir el gasto o consumo de algo.
tip: He needs to reduce his spending to save money.

sentence: The manager decided to take ______ two new members of staff.
explanation: El phrasal verb <take on> significa contratar a alguien o aceptar una responsabilidad.
tip: The manager hired two new employees.

sentence: They ran ______ of supplies halfway through the expedition.
explanation: El phrasal verb <run out of> significa quedarse sin algo.
tip: They had no more supplies left during the expedition.

sentence: She drew ______ her years of experience to solve the problem.
explanation: El phrasal verb <draw on> significa utilizar una habilidad o experiencia.
tip: She used her experience as a resource to find a solution.

sentence: The effect of the medication wore ______ after a few hours.
explanation: El phrasal verb <wear off> significa desaparecer gradualmente.
tip: The medication stopped working after a few hours.

sentence: He stood ______ from the other candidates thanks to his unique skills.
explanation: El phrasal verb <stand out> significa destacar o ser notablemente diferente.
tip: He was clearly better or different from the other candidates.

sentence: The company plans to roll ______ the new app next month.
explanation: El phrasal verb <roll out> significa lanzar o poner a disposición del público.
tip: The company will launch the app to the public next month.

sentence: She had to get ______ her fear of public speaking.
explanation: El phrasal verb <get over> significa superar o recuperarse de algo.
tip: She overcame her fear and became comfortable speaking in public.

sentence: The government decided to bring ______ new environmental regulations.
explanation: El phrasal verb <bring in> significa introducir o implementar algo nuevo.
tip: The government introduced new rules to protect the environment.

sentence: He stepped ______ and took charge when the leader was absent.
explanation: El phrasal verb <step up> significa asumir más responsabilidad o esforzarse más.
tip: He took on more responsibility when the leader wasn't there.

sentence: The detective looked ______ the suspicious activity reported by neighbours.
explanation: El phrasal verb <look into> significa investigar algo.
tip: The detective investigated the suspicious activity.

sentence: She came ______ an old letter while sorting through her grandmother's belongings.
explanation: El phrasal verb <come across> significa encontrar algo por casualidad.
tip: She found the letter unexpectedly while sorting things.

sentence: The company had to lay ______ fifty workers due to financial difficulties.
explanation: El phrasal verb <lay off> significa despedir a empleados por razones económicas.
tip: The company dismissed workers because of financial problems.

sentence: He found it hard to keep ______ with the pace of the advanced class.
explanation: El phrasal verb <keep up with> significa mantener el mismo ritmo o nivel.
tip: He struggled to stay at the same level as the rest of the class.

sentence: She put ______ a proposal to improve the company's sustainability practices.
explanation: El phrasal verb <put forward> significa proponer o presentar formalmente una idea.
tip: She formally proposed an idea to improve sustainability.

sentence: The new CEO brought ______ significant changes to the company culture.
explanation: El phrasal verb <bring about> significa causar o provocar un cambio.
tip: The new CEO caused significant changes in the company.

sentence: He backed ______ from his original position after seeing the evidence.
explanation: El phrasal verb <back down> significa retractarse o ceder en una discusión.
tip: He changed his position after seeing the evidence.

sentence: She managed to get ______ the difficult period with support from her friends.
explanation: El phrasal verb <get through> significa superar o sobrevivir una situación difícil.
tip: She survived the difficult period thanks to her friends' support.

sentence: The team held ______ their best players for the final match.
explanation: El phrasal verb <hold back> significa retener o reservar algo para más tarde.
tip: They saved their best players for the most important game.

sentence: He found ______ the truth about the project only after it was too late.
explanation: El phrasal verb <find out> significa descubrir o enterarse de algo.
tip: He discovered the truth, but it was already too late.

sentence: The company set ______ a new branch in the city centre.
explanation: El phrasal verb <set up> significa establecer o crear algo nuevo.
tip: The company established a new office in the city centre.

sentence: She had to go ______ a lengthy approval process before starting the project.
explanation: El phrasal verb <go through> significa pasar por un proceso o experiencia.
tip: She had to complete a long approval process before starting.
